# Extract link resolution into standalone package

<!-- Generated by seed-golden-context | Last updated: 2026-05-07 -->

## Status

Accepted

## Context

The Contentful JavaScript SDK (`contentful.js`) contained inline logic for resolving links in CDA responses. This logic was growing in complexity (handling circular references, multi-type includes, nested fields) and was tightly coupled to the SDK.

The initial commit message reads: "Moved from contentful.js" (commit `0eb566e`, 2014-06-06).

## Decision

Extract the link resolution logic into a standalone npm package (`contentful-resolve-response`) that:
- Takes a raw CDA response as input and returns resolved items
- Has no dependency on the SDK's internal architecture
- Can be consumed independently by developers who use raw HTTP against the CDA

## Consequences

- The SDK can depend on this package without circular coupling
- The resolution logic can be tested and versioned independently
- Direct consumers get link resolution without pulling in the full SDK
- The library must maintain backward compatibility since it is a public transitive dependency of `contentful.js`
